_description: Accessible motion tokens. All animation values are designed with prefers-reduced-motion in mind. WCAG 2.3.3 (AAA) and 2.2.2 (A) apply.

duration._note: Use these as the default duration. Always provide a prefers-reduced-motion override.

slow.note: Larger reveals: modal open, drawer slide

ease-out.note: Recommended for elements entering the screen

ease-in-out.note: Recommended for elements moving within the screen

reduced-motion._note: When @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) is active, apply these values instead of the standard duration/easing.

duration-override.note: Near-instant — animation technically fires but is imperceptible. Safer than 0ms which can break JS animation callbacks.

reduced-motion.note: Pattern: use transition-duration: var(--duration-normal); and override to var(--duration-reduced) in the media query.

css-pattern._note: Copy-paste CSS pattern for reduced motion:
css-pattern.example: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, *::before, *::after { animation-duration: 0.01ms !important; animation-iteration-count: 1 !important; transition-duration: 0.01ms !important; scroll-behavior: auto !important; } }

autoplay._note: WCAG 2.2.2: Content that moves for more than 5 seconds and starts automatically must have a pause/stop/hide mechanism.

max-autoplay-duration.note: Content that autoplays beyond 5s needs a pause control
